edges.description: Pathogenic MECP2 variants disrupt the DNA-binding and transcriptional-regulatory functions of MeCP2.
Different recurrent MECP2 mutations modify the expected severity of motor, hand-use, and language phenotypes.
Loss of MeCP2 regulatory function changes expression of many neuronal genes.
Dysregulated neuronal gene expression affects synapse and dendrite maturation.

nodes.description: MECP2-mutant neurons show altered calcium signaling and electrophysiological defects, indicating impaired activity-dependent network maturation. This network-level dysfunction connects synaptic defects to seizures, motor abnormalities, hand stereotypies, developmental regression, and impaired cognition.
nodes.id: Altered Neuronal Calcium Signaling and Electrophysiology

evidence.reference_title: A model for neural development and treatment of Rett syndrome using human induced pluripotent stem cells.
evidence.snippet: Neurons derived from RTT-iPSCs had fewer synapses, reduced spine density, smaller soma size, altered calcium signaling and electrophysiological defects when compared to controls.

nodes.description: Breathing disturbances arise from MeCP2-dependent dysfunction in respiratory control networks, including ventrolateral medulla and pontine circuits.

evidence.reference_title: Breathing challenges in Rett syndrome: lessons learned from humans and animal models.
evidence.snippet: Research in mouse models of RTT suggests that different areas in the ventrolateral medulla and pons give rise to different aspects of this breathing disorder.

evidence.reference_title: Rett syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Affected individuals may present multiple other neurological impairments and comorbidities, such as seizures, breathing irregularities, anxiety and constipation.

evidence.reference_title: Altered gut microbiota in Rett syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Rett syndrome (RTT), a progressive neurological disorder mainly caused by mutations in MeCP2 gene, is commonly associated with gastrointestinal dysfunctions and constipation, suggesting a link between RTT's gastrointestinal abnormalities and the gut microbiota.

evidence.reference_title: Characterizing the journey of Rett syndrome among females in the United States: a real-world evidence study using the Rett syndrome natural history study database
evidence.snippet: A total of 455 females with RTT were included in the study, of whom 90.5% had classic RTT and 79.8% were pediatric individuals.

nodes.description: MeCP2 normally binds methylated CpG dinucleotides and participates in transcriptional repression and activation. Loss or altered dosage of MeCP2 changes neuronal gene-expression programs, providing the proximal molecular bridge from MECP2 mutation to downstream neurodevelopmental defects.

evidence.reference_title: Rett syndrome is caused by mutations in X-linked MECP2, encoding methyl-CpG-binding protein 2.
evidence.snippet: MeCP2 selectively binds CpG dinucleotides in the mammalian genome and mediates transcriptional repression through interaction with histone deacetylase and the corepressor SIN3A

evidence.reference_title: MeCP2, a key contributor to neurological disease, activates and represses transcription.
evidence.snippet: These studies suggest that MeCP2 regulates the expression of a wide range of genes in the hypothalamus and that it can function as both an activator and a repressor of transcription.

evidence.reference_title: Rett syndrome.
evidence.snippet: RTT is characterized by developmental regression of spoken language and hand use that, with hand stereotypies and impaired ambulation, constitute the four core diagnostic features.

nodes.description: De novo and inherited pathogenic variants in the X-linked MECP2 gene disrupt methyl-CpG-binding protein 2. Reported Rett-causing variant classes include methyl-binding-domain missense substitutions and frameshift or nonsense variants that disrupt the transcription repression domain.

nodes.description: Oral synthetic glycine-proline-glutamate analog supported by a phase 3 randomized trial for improvement in core Rett syndrome symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Trofinetide for the treatment of Rett syndrome: a randomized phase 3 study.
evidence.snippet: Significant improvement for trofinetide compared with placebo was observed for the coprimary efficacy endpoints, suggesting that trofinetide provides benefit in treating the core symptoms of Rett syndrome.

nodes.description: Recurrent MECP2 variants alter the severity profile of the downstream phenotype. R168X and large deletions are associated with greater severity, while R133C, R294X, and carboxy-terminal truncations are associated with milder motor, hand-use, language, or scoliosis risk profiles in cohort studies.

evidence.reference_title: Specific mutations in methyl-CpG-binding protein 2 confer different severity in Rett syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Specific mutations in MECP2 confer different severity.

evidence.reference_title: Specific mutations in methyl-CpG-binding protein 2 confer different severity in Rett syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Clinical differences are notable in ambulation, hand use, and language (p < 0.004), three cardinal features of Rett syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Profiling scoliosis in Rett syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Two common methyl-CpG-binding protein 2 (MECP2) mutations, R294X and R306C, had reduced risk for scoliosis.
